authoritative_sources.title: Workforce Planning Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority. Its workforce-planning toolkit defines demand, supply, gap, and action-planning stages that constrain recommendation-safe event scheduling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: demand framing, capacity-gap analysis, and action-plan packaging

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DOJ is the primary federal authority for ADA public-accommodation enforcement. Its ticket-sales guidance is the controlling public baseline for accessible seating sales, hold and release behavior, and comparable booking conditions for event venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking prerequisite review and accessible-inventory control

authoritative_sources.title: Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. federal consumer-protection authority. Its fee-rule FAQ explicitly covers live-event ticketing and total-price disclosure, which directly constrains pricing-aware booking recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee-aware booking review and guest-facing price disclosure boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: BOTS Act compliance time: A refresher on compliance with the Better Online Ticket Sales Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC enforces the Better Online Ticket Sales Act and its public business guidance explains anti-bot and posted ticket-limit boundaries that matter when suspicious onsale activity changes scheduling or guest-communication posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive onsale handling and suspicious booking escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator. Its billing-error guidance anchors the timeline and issuer-versus-merchant boundary when ticketing, deposits, or add-on fees are disputed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch handling and refund-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: How do I get my money back after I discover an unauthorized transaction or money missing from my bank account?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB explains consumer timelines and investigation handling for unauthorized transactions, which define when the lane must stop and route suspected payment fraud rather than improvise a guest-service resolution.

authoritative_sources.title: Mass Gatherings - Security Awareness for Soft Targets and Crowded Places
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the primary U.S. federal critical-infrastructure security agency. Its mass-gatherings guidance provides public risk, stakeholder, suspicious-activity, and emergency-preparedness expectations that directly affect whether an event is schedule-ready.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: site-readiness review and security-aware event escalation

authoritative_sources.title: ESA Guides
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Event Safety Alliance is a recognized industry body for live-event safety practice. Its public guidance set is directly relevant to event scheduling because it ties site readiness, weather, communications, crowd management, and guest-safe execution to schedule decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event-readiness review, contingency planning, and live-event terminology grounding

workflow_stages.name: Demand and event-intake normalization
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the request is actually arts, entertainment, or recreation event scheduling and normalize hold status, event type, venue slot, onsale posture, staffing need, and planning horizon into one scheduling frame.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Booking truth-set and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm event brief, booking or reservation record, payment or deposit state, accessible inventory posture, staffing minimums, tenant tie-break rules, and required owner map before a date or routing commitment is treated as safe.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess venue turns, load-in and load-out windows, labor and contractor availability, weather contingencies, site-readiness blockers, security dependencies, and approved service-level limits before recommending changes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, sequence, and allocation recommendation support
workflow_stages.description: Prepare recommendation-only date, turn, staffing, queue, and fallback sequencing outputs using approved tie-break rules and named event artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake and live-event readiness refresh
workflow_stages.description: Triage onsales, weekend surges, waitlist growth, weather watch, and overlapping venue turns while preserving record checks, guest-impact awareness, and authority boundaries.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception and risk escalation
workflow_stages.description: Keep refund, fee-waiver, payment-dispute, bot-activity, suspicious-activity, and crowd-safety issues explicit and route them to the authorized owner when they determine the answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: event briefs, booking holds, confirmed dates, rehearsal windows, run-of-show drafts, manifests, and venue-calendar entries
ticketing records, reservation records, accessible seating inventory states, onsale calendars, ticket allotments, and deposit or payment records
staffing minimums, crew and contractor rosters, security and usher assignments, volunteer schedules, and approved overtime posture
load-in and load-out plans, changeover buffers, site maps, ingress and egress plans, weather contingencies, and maintenance or outage notices
refund policies, postponement rules, service-recovery notes, disputed payment references, suspicious-order notes, and loss-prevention cases
approval-needed summaries, escalation notes, and owner routing maps
systems_of_record: service desk or guest recovery tracker
event production or venue calendar system

allowed: Classify event scheduling work as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Assess whether available records support a bounded schedule, routing, booking-readiness, or allocation recommendation.
Produce evidence-backed scheduling recommendations, readiness summaries, constraint logs, and escalation packages without claiming final approval authority.
Route work to ticketing, guest services, pricing, fraud, venue operations, production, or safety owners when the determining authority sits outside the lane.
escalate: Required booking, ticketing, payment, accessible inventory, staffing, site-readiness, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires refund exception approval, pricing override, fraud investigation, security action, permit interpretation, or final operational approval.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, promoter, venue, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict on accessible seating, fee disclosure, hold release, weather handling, or emergency posture.
A guest-impacting payment dispute, suspected bot activity, suspicious-activity signal, or crowd-safety issue changes whether the event can proceed as scheduled.
refuse: Present a planning recommendation as a final date lock, onsale release, staffing approval, budget approval, refund approval, or operational signoff.
Override pricing, refund, fee, ticket-limit, or safety policy without explicit authority.
Treat generic calendar entries or sales forecasts as sufficient substitutes for booking holds, ticketing records, accessible inventory state, staffing minimums, and site-readiness evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Event demand is committed against stale or conflicting hold, booking, or deposit records.
Accessible seating inventory is released or scheduled without the authoritative hold and release posture.
Weather, crowd-safety, or suspicious-activity signals are ignored to preserve the planned date.
Refund, fee-waiver, or service-recovery pressure is treated as scheduling authority rather than routed to the correct owner.
Bot-driven or unauthorized-ticket activity distorts demand and guest communication decisions.
Venue turns, load-in windows, staffing minimums, or changeover constraints are treated as soft assumptions instead of hard blockers.
Tenant-specific promoter, venue, or jurisdiction rules are skipped and replaced with generic event language.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Refund rights for postponement, lineup change, or partial cancellation vary by tenant contract and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Not blocking because the pack requires tenant-supplied refund and exception policy before any recommendation that depends on those rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Crowd-management, permit, and emergency-services staffing thresholds vary by venue type and local authority.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Not blocking because the lane refuses safety-signoff authority and requires venue- and jurisdiction-specific operating limits at runtime.

notes: The research contract is satisfied with authoritative sources covering workflow, booking and pricing posture, payment mismatch handling, fraud escalation, and event-safety readiness.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant and jurisdiction variance is explicitly surfaced as required runtime input rather than guessed.
